title: wattakaka sinensis var. corrugata
intro: Wattakaka sinensis var. corrugata, also known as the Thai waxflower or Chinese calotropis, is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive waxy texture and corrugated leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of calotropin and other cardioactive glycosides. The health benefits of Wattakaka sinensis var. corrugata include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
